name: Malmstrom AFB - Echo Flight (1967)
description: On March 16, 1967, 10 Minuteman ICBMs went offline simultaneously. Captain Robert Salas reported a glowing red object hovering over the flight security controller while the shutdowns occurred. Corroborated by multiple officers.

name: Minot AFB - B-52 Incident (1968)
description: October 1968. A UFO tracked by ground radar was observed near B-52 bomber and ICBM launch facilities. Crew reported the object on multiple instruments. One of the most thoroughly documented military cases.

name: Loring AFB (1975)
description: October-November 1975. Unidentified helicopter-like craft overflew the nuclear weapons storage area on multiple nights. Military police and base commanders filed reports. Part of the 1975 northern tier intrusion series.

name: Wurtsmith AFB (1975)
description: October 1975. Part of the same northern tier intrusion series as Loring and Minot. Unidentified aerial objects tracked near nuclear storage. NORAD alerted multiple times.

name: F.E. Warren AFB (1980)
description: August 1980. Security personnel in ICBM maintenance tunnels reported large glowing objects hovering over the facility. Filed official incident reports. Adjacent to Cheyenne, WY.

name: Hanford Site (1945-1950s)
description: Multiple UAP reports over Hanford plutonium production reactors beginning in 1945. FBI and AEC documents released via FOIA detail recurring unidentified objects over restricted airspace. Among the earliest post-war nuclear UAP reports.

name: Indian Point Nuclear Plant (1984)
description: July 24, 1984. A large V-shaped or boomerang craft reported by multiple security personnel and civilians hovering directly over the Indian Point nuclear reactor in New York. Security attempted to intercept. FAA filed reports.
